cit.basis: Flat 12.5% corporate income tax (Income Tax Law 118(I)/2002, as amended) on worldwide profits of Cyprus tax-resident companies.

ip_box.note: IP box: 80% exemption on qualifying IP profits under the nexus approach, giving an effective rate as low as 2.5%. Out of scope for this SME estimator.

tonnage_tax.note: Qualifying shipping companies may elect tonnage tax instead of CIT under the Merchant Shipping (Fees and Taxing Provisions) Law. Out of scope for this SME estimator.

cit.reform_note: A 15% CIT rate has been announced as part of the Cyprus tax reform, but is not yet law within the target period (2025).

sdc.note: Special Defence Contribution on passive income of Cyprus tax-resident and domiciled persons: dividends 17%, most interest 17%, rents on 75% of gross at 3%. Non-domiciled residents are generally exempt.

vat.note: Standard VAT 19% with reduced rates of 9%, 5% and 3%. As an EU member state, Cyprus participates in the One Stop Shop (OSS) for intra-EU B2C supplies.

pillar_two.note: Cyprus transposed the EU Pillar Two directive (Council Directive (EU) 2022/2523): a 15% minimum effective rate applies to in-scope groups with consolidated revenue of at least EUR 750 million. Out of scope for this SME estimator.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
IP box, tonnage tax, SDC, notional interest deduction, loss relief, and treaty rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
